title: Sort It Out
editorialSummary: Sorting algorithms grouped by the underlying mechanism they use. Comparison-based divide-and-conquer: Merge Sort, Quick Sort, Tim Sort, Intro Sort — all recursively partition or merge. Insertion-based: Insertion Sort, Shell Sort, Library Sort, Binary Insertion Sort — build a sorted portion one element at a time. Selection-based: Selection Sort, Heap Sort, Cycle Sort, Smooth Sort — repeatedly select the minimum (or maximum). Distribution / non-comparison: Radix Sort, Counting Sort, Bucket Sort, Pigeonhole Sort — sort without element comparisons, using digit or value distribution. False trail: Heap Sort looks like divide-and-conquer but is canonically selection-based (repeatedly selects the heap maximum). Tim Sort looks like insertion (because it uses insertion for small runs) but is classified as divide-and-conquer merge-based.
